prior_sessions_summary: 2026-06-14: diagnosis only — identified the two coupled cheats (2 pins + 1 regfix subst); floor 10 (masked, unreliable here).

sessions.lever: full analysis + empirical confirmation. (1) RA: GCC priority formula from global.c:604 = floor_log2(n_refs)*n_refs/live_length*10k. result(n=6,len=400)=300 > sel2(n=3,len=124)=242 â†’ result takes $s2. Empirically: pins removed + regfix kept = SHA1 46e36200 (RA mismatch). (2) Cross-jump: minimum-2 fires when case9/11 uses sel=0xD (both blocks `li s0,13; j sel_dispatch`). Mixed-exit-forms lever not applicable (neither path can return mid-function). No pure-C lever found for either issue.

sessions.rationale: Owner ruling: B (send-back). Cluster precedent (saEft00Add / marionation_Exec / cpu_side_move_dir_4 / func_8007C97C) is circumstantially strong but does not meet the canonical-asm bar — Lever D demonstrates bytes are C-reachable. HEAD's 2 register pins + 1 regfix retained as cheat-infrastructure until clean replacement lands.

sessions.lever: WALL 1 SOLVED — duplicated-store priority lift: load_sel2's store duplicated into case 0 (FAKE-annotated), label moved to case 13/17's copy. Real second def bumps sel2 reg_n_refs 3->4 (pri 851 > result 412) -> RA lands sel2->$s2/result->$s3 naturally; jump2 cross-jump re-merges the tails (zero bytes; label placement steers merge direction — mirror labeling emits a 4-diff layout). Both pins RETIRED; function byte-diff vs canonical EMPTY; full SHA1 == oracle with the 1 rule applied. Dead stores measured INERT for global RA (flow deletes without counting). Wall 2 attempts m7 (label aliasing — jump1 unifies) and m8 (two-goto arm reshape) both still merge; open lead = REG_DEAD-note equality requirement in find_cross_jump.

reviewer.evidence_2026_07_01: Layer-2 FAIL on family-scope (mechanics verified: byte-diff empty, SHA1==oracle, one physical lbu in final bytes). Appealed with fresh SOTN evidence (cross-arm duplication routine in matched code); owner sanctioned duplicated-statement-into-arms.md. Prior history below.
reviewer.evidence: Two coupled forbidden cheats: (1) register pins result asm("s3")/sel2 asm("s2"); (2) regfix subst 'addiu $16,$zero,12'->'13' @149 (commented 'anti-cross-jump fix'). Removing pins flips RA (masked-invisible); removing the rule via setting case9/11 sel=0xD FAILS the full build. Likely needs cross-jump-defeating restructure + a pure-C RA lever, or canonical-asm auth. Session 2 (2026-06-16): exhaustive analysis confirms BOTH issues are plateaus â€” no pure-C lever exists for either.

remaining_gap.summary: 402-insn dispatcher. Target: result in $s3, sel2 in $s2. Without pins: resultâ†’$s2, sel2â†’$s3 (SHA1 46e36200). Without regfix (case9/11 sel=0xD): cross_jump merges ea0+fc0 blocks (SHA1 82e50f76). GCC priority formula (global.c:604) shows result (priorityâ‰ˆ300) > sel2 (priorityâ‰ˆ242): sel2 needs n_refsâ‰¥4 to win, but any 4th ref changes the binary (oracle has li a1,-1 not move a1,s2). Cross_jump minimum-2 fires with sel=0xD for case9/11. No pure-C solution for either issue found after exhaustive analysis.
remaining_gap.rules_required_for_full_match: HEAD byte-matches ONLY via 2 cheat-asm pins + 1 regfix subst. No pure-C form found after exhaustive analysis of RA priority formula and cross_jump mechanism.

next_hypotheses: UN-TRIED — attack Wall 1 (RA priority) via emission-byte-neutral changes: try (a) restructuring the case-9/11 result-setting blocks so the 4 `result=1;` stores come from a single shared assignment reached by goto, but cross-jump RESPREADS into 4 emissions (this is the inverse of the wall — make the C show 1 store, let cross-jump spread it). (b) Examine whether ANY structural change to the case-bodies that doesn't touch result/sel2 nrefs/livelen *indirectly* shifts pri via livelen recomputation cascade.

UN-TRIED — attack Wall 2 (cross-jump suffix): the documented `cross-jump-store-tail-merge` mix-exit lever was ruled out because both paths must reach the shared sel_dispatch call. But there's an UN-EXAMINED variant — restructuring so the SUFFIX (not the exit) differs. E.g., introduce a no-op USE/CLOBBER that survives prev_nonnote_insn but breaks rtx_renumbered_equal_p at exactly insn #2 of the suffix. Note: must NOT emit bytes (USE/CLOBBER are skipped per jump.c:2524, so the trick is a construct that survives the skip but breaks the rtx comparison). This is at the edge of what's structurally possible — un-tested.

UN-TRIED — combined attack: change the sel2 initialization site so it's BORN later (after early-exit) AND restructure the post-early-exit code so target's prologue `addiu $s2,-1` is reproduced naturally via a different sequencing. Adjudicator ruled this out under livelen analysis BUT — Lever C variant 'loop-local precompute' was waved off as 'no loop here'; check whether the case-dispatch can be reshaped as a logical loop-1-iteration for sel2 lifetime purposes.

Latent policy deliberation: if cluster grows + permuter benchmark + SOTN cross-check support it, canonical-asm signal set may expand to include 'documented N-function cluster with empirical RA-priority + cross-jump coupling'. Not actionable per-function; surface to owner separately if cluster reaches 6+ members.
